perturbation.original_text: 1. EXTENSION OF SERVICE AGREEMENT. Subject to all other terms and conditions set forth herein, as of the date of this agreement, LEADER maintain the software for an additional five years.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. EXTENSION OF SERVICE AGREEMENT. Subject to all other terms and conditions set forth herein, as of the date of this agreement, LEADER will maintain the software for an additional five years, with an option for EZJR to terminate this agreement at any time with 30 days written notice.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by granting EZJR the right to terminate the maintenance agreement with 30 days' notice, which contradicts the initially stated five-year extension in section 1 and creates uncertainty. It contradicts the expectation that the agreement extends for a fixed five-year term, potentially conflicting with the compensation terms outlined in section 2, which assumes a fixed period. This could lead to disputes over the value of the stock issued if EZJR terminates early.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. COMPENSATION TO LEADER. The compensation to LEADER shall be: 3,000,000 shares of EZJR's unregistered restricted common stock which shall be issuable upon the closing;
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clause allowing EZJR to terminate the agreement with 30 days' notice, directly contradicting the original agreement for a fixed five-year extension. This contradicts section 2, which compensates LEADER with stock issuable 'upon the closing,' implying a completed five-year term. The termination clause creates uncertainty about whether LEADER will receive the full value of the stock if EZJR terminates early, leading to a direct in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3. INDEMNIFICATION AND SURVIVAL. All representations and warranties made in this Agreement shall survive the Closing of this Agreement, except that any party to whom a representation or warranty has been made in this Agreement shall be deemed to have waived any misrepresentation or breach of representation or warranty of which such party had knowledge prior to Closing. Any party learning of a misrepresentation or breach of representation or warranty under this Agreement shall immediately give written notice thereof to all other parties to this Agreement. The representations and warranties in this Agreement shall terminate one year from the Closing Date, and such representations or warranties shall thereafter be without force or effect, except any claim with respect to which notice has been given to the party to be charged prior to such expiration date. LEADER hereby agrees to indemnify and hold EZJR, it successors, and assigns harmless from and against any and all damage or deficiency resulting from any material misrepresentation, breach of warranty or covenant, or nonfulfillment of any agreement on the part of LEADER under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. INDEMNIFICATION AND SURVIVAL. All representations and warranties made in this Agreement shall survive the Closing of this Agreement, except that any party to whom a representation or warranty has been made in this Agreement shall be deemed to have waived any misrepresentation or breach of representation or warranty of which such party had knowledge prior to Closing. Any party learning of a misrepresentation or breach of representation or warranty under this Agreement shall immediately give written notice thereof to all other parties to this Agreement. The representations and warranties in this Agreement shall terminate one year from the Closing Date, and such representations or warranties shall thereafter be without force or effect, except any claim with respect to which notice has been given to the party to be charged prior to such expiration date. LEADER hereby agrees to indemnify and hold EZJR, it successors, and assigns harmless from and against any and all damage or deficiency resulting from any material misrepresentation, breach of warranty or covenant, or nonfulfillment of any agreement on the part of LEADER under this Agreement. However, LEADER's indemnification obligations shall not exceed $10,000 in the aggregate.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by limiting LEADER's indemnification obligations to a maximum of $10,000. While the original text states that LEADER will indemnify EZJR against 'any and all damage or deficiency,' the added limitation creates uncertainty and conflict in enforcement. This contradicts the original intent of providing full indemnification and could lead to disputes over the extent of LEADER's liability for breaches or misrepresentations. It directly contradicts the phrase 'any and all damage or deficiency'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. INDEMNIFICATION AND SURVIVAL. All representations and warranties made in this Agreement shall survive the Closing of this Agreement, except that any party to whom a representation or warranty has been made in this Agreement shall be deemed to have waived any misrepresentation or breach of representation or warranty of which such party had knowledge prior to Closing. Any party learning of a misrepresentation or breach of representation or warranty under this Agreement shall immediately give written notice thereof to all other parties to this Agreement. The representations and warranties in this Agreement shall terminate one year from the Closing Date, and such representations or warranties shall thereafter be without force or effect, except any claim with respect to which notice has been given to the party to be charged prior to such expiration date. LEADER hereby agrees to indemnify and hold EZJR, it successors, and assigns harmless from and against any and all damage or deficiency resulting from any material misrepresentation, breach of warranty or covenant, or nonfulfillment of any agreement on the part of LEADER under this Agreement. However, LEADER's indemnification obligations shall not exceed $10,000 in the aggregate.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that LEADER will indemnify EZJR against 'any and all damage or deficiency'. The changed text adds a limitation to LEADER's indemnification obligations, capping them at $10,000 in the aggregate. This directly contradicts the original promise of indemnification for 'any and all damage or deficiency', creating an in-text contradiction. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type where different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 11. ATTORNEYS' FEES. Each party will pay its or his own legal fees and other expenses in connection with the preparation of this Agreement and the sale of Assets in accordance with this Agreement. However, if any legal action or other proceeding is brought for the enforcement of this Agreement, or because or arising out of an alleged dispute, breach, default or misrepresentation in connection with any of the provisions of this Agreement, the prevailing party will be entitled to recover reasonable attorney's fees and other costs incurred in that action or proceeding, in addition to any other relief to which it or he may be entitled.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. ATTORNEYS' FEES. Each party will pay its or his own legal fees and other expenses in connection with the preparation of this Agreement and the sale of Assets in accordance with this Agreement. However, if any legal action or other proceeding is brought for the enforcement of this Agreement, or because or arising out of an alleged dispute, breach, default or misrepresentation in connection with any of the provisions of this Agreement, the prevailing party will be entitled to recover reasonable attorney's fees and other costs incurred in that action or proceeding, in addition to any other relief to which it or he may be entitled. Notwithstanding the foregoing, in no event shall the attorney's fees awarded to the prevailing party exceed $5,000.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction within the attorney's fees clause (Section 11). While the original text states that the prevailing party is entitled to recover 'reasonable attorney's fees,' the added sentence caps the attorney's fees at $5,000, regardless of the reasonableness or actual amount incurred. This creates a contradiction because it is unclear whether the prevailing party is entitled to all reasonable fees (as initially stated) or is limited to the $5,000 cap. This could lead to disputes over the actual amount of attorney's fees recoverable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. ATTORNEYS' FEES. Each party will pay its or his own legal fees and other expenses in connection with the preparation of this Agreement and the sale of Assets in accordance with this Agreement. However, if any legal action or other proceeding is brought for the enforcement of this Agreement, or because or arising out of an alleged dispute, breach, default or misrepresentation in connection with any of the provisions of this Agreement, the prevailing party will be entitled to recover reasonable attorney's fees and other costs incurred in that action or proceeding, in addition to any other relief to which it or he may be entitled. Notwithstanding the foregoing, in no event shall the attorney's fees awarded to the prevailing party exceed $5,000.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 11 to include a cap of $5,000 on attorney's fees, while the original section entitled the prevailing party to recover 'reasonable attorney's fees'. Since the changed text and contradicted text are the same, but the change introduces a conflict between 'reasonable' fees and a capped amount, this creates a direct, in-text contradiction. This directly adheres to the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction', as the attorney's fees clause now includes conflicting obligations.
